summary:
Water has an uneven electron distribution due to its bent shape and high electronegativity of oxygen, leading to a permanent dipole. Oxygen gas (O₂) and liquid nitrogen (N₂) are nonpolar due to their symmetrical, homonuclear diatomic structures with equal atom-sharing and canceling bond dipoles.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{D}$

Oxygen gas (O₂) and liquid nitrogen (N₂) are nonpolar due to their symmetrical, homonuclear diatomic structures with equal atom-sharing and canceling bond dipoles.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{D}$. Answer choice: D
